pi_items.verified_value: Original source: https://download.blender.org/demo/test/classroom.zip, extracted to /home/ga/BlenderDemos/classroom/classroom.blend, then copied to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/vr_classroom.blend
pi_items.source: setup_blender.sh line 49 (downloads from official Blender server) and setup_task.sh line 8-24 (copies demo to working location)
Google VR documentation (developers.google.com/vr/discover/360-degree-media) and equirectangular format specifications confirm 2:1 aspect ratio is standard for monoscopic equirectangular panoramas
Blender manual (docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/render/cycles/object_settings/cameras.html) specifies Cycles is required for panoramic equirectangular rendering

Blender manual confirms panoramic camera type is required for 360° panoramic rendering. Documentation states: 'set your render engine to Cycles Render, then select your camera and in the Lens section choose Panoramic'
Blender Cycles camera documentation and VR specification standards (Google VR, design4real.de) confirm equirectangular is the correct panorama projection type for VR 360° content

Cannot verify without accessing Blender interpreter or .blend file binary. The value is reasonable (1.5m is standard head height for standing VR viewer; -5.0, 0.0 appears to be room-center positioning), but exact position not confirmed
Task setup scripts (setup_task.sh, export_result.sh) reference this path as expected output file for saved Blender project with VR panorama configuration
Task setup and export scripts reference this path as the expected rendered image output from the panoramic camera

Official Blender demo server, confirmed through setup_blender.sh line 49 and downloaded/verified on 2026-03-07. File metadata: 68.2 MB, dated 2019-06-13, CC0 license (public domain). Created by Christophe Seux.
Blender official demo files page confirms classroom.blend is under CC0 license, making it freely usable for any purpose

privileged_info_summary: Task uses real, publicly available data from official Blender demo files. The classroom.blend file is a professionally created interior rendering scene from 2019 (72 MB, CC0 license) available at https://download.blender.org/demo/test/classroom.zip. All technical specifications for VR panoramic rendering are verified as correct: equirectangular projection with 2:1 aspect ratio (2048x1024) is the standard for monoscopic VR content; Cycles render engine with panoramic camera type and equirectangular projection are the correct Blender settings documented in official manuals. Camera location [-5.0, 0.0, 1.5] appears reasonable (1.5m is standard VR head height) but could not be independently verified without Blender runtime. Target paths for output files are correctly specified in task scripts.
